en: That's two days away.
Look, honey, the kids are exhausted, you are exhausted.
ko: 자, 자기야, 아이들이 지쳤고, 당신도 지쳤어.

en: There's absolutely nothing more that we can do in this airport.
ko: 이 공항에서 더 이상 할 수 있는 게 없어요.

en: Now, I say we go over to Rob's,
and that way we can call the police again, and they can get back to us.
ko: 그렇게 하면 경찰에 다시 연락할 수 있고, 그들이 우리에게 연락할 수 있어.

en: Peter, Kevin is home all by himself.
I'm not leaving here unless it's on an airplane.
ko: 비행기를 타지 않는 한 여기 떠나지 않을 거야.

en: Madam, we are doing everything we can.
Now, if you want to stay at the airport,
maybe we can get you on standby.
It is a possibility that the seat will open up. Okay? Yes. I'll wait.
ko: 좌석이 열릴 가능성이 있어요. 알겠죠? 네, 기다릴게요.

en: Everything's full.
Everything's full.
I'm very sorry, but it is Christmas Eve.
What about another airline?
Nothing available.
May I help you get a hotel room in the city?
Tomorrow afternoon we can get you a flight to Chicago.
ko: 내일 오후에 시카고행 비행기를 예약해 드릴 수 있어요.

en: I can't wait that long.
I'm terribly sorry, ma'am,
but we're doing absolutely everything we can. Go ahead.
ko: 하지만 저희는 최선을 다하고 있습니다. 계속하세요.

en: I'm sorry.
Excuse me. You have places to go, people to see. Got a ticket there.
ko: 실례합니다. 가야 할 곳이 있고, 만날 사람이 있죠. 거기 티켓이 있어요.
